WriteLn.description: Writes one or more values to standard output followed by a newline character. Accepts mixed types including strings, integers, reals, and booleans with optional field width formatting specifiers.

ReadLn.description: Reads a line of input from standard input, assigning values to the specified variables and consuming the newline character. Used for interactive console input in Pascal programs.

Write.description: Writes one or more values to standard output without appending a newline, allowing multiple write calls to produce a single output line before the cursor remains on the same line.

Integer.description: The standard signed integer type in Pascal, typically 16-bit in Turbo Pascal or 32-bit in Free Pascal and Delphi. Supports arithmetic operations, comparisons, and ordinal functions.

String.description: The character string type in Pascal, storing a sequence of characters with a length byte prefix. Default maximum length is 255 characters. Supports concatenation, comparison, and index access.

Length.description: Returns the number of characters currently stored in the string.

Copy.signature: Copy(s: String, index: Integer, count: Integer) -> String
Copy.description: Returns a substring from s starting at index for count characters.

Pos.description: Returns the position of the first occurrence of substr in s, or 0 if not found.

Delete.signature: Delete(var s: String, index: Integer, count: Integer)
Delete.description: Removes count characters from string s starting at the specified index.

Insert.signature: Insert(source: String, var s: String, index: Integer)
Insert.description: Inserts the source string into s at the specified index position.

Real.description: The floating-point numeric type in Pascal for storing decimal numbers. Typically 48-bit in Turbo Pascal or mapped to Double (64-bit) in Free Pascal and Delphi for IEEE 754 compliance.

Boolean.description: The logical type in Pascal with exactly two values: True and False. Used in conditional expressions, control flow statements, and as function return types for predicate operations.

Array.description: A fixed-size collection of elements of the same type, indexed by an ordinal type range. Arrays in Pascal are declared with explicit bounds and support multidimensional and packed variants.

Low.description: Returns the lowest valid index value for the array.

High.description: Returns the highest valid index value for the array.

Length.description: Returns the number of elements in the array (or dynamic array).

SetLength.description: Resizes a dynamic array to the specified length, preserving existing elements.

Record.signature: Record field1: Type1; field2: Type2; end
Record.description: A composite data type that groups related fields of different types under a single name. Records support variant parts, can be nested, and are used for structured data modeling in Pascal.

TStringList.description: A powerful string collection class in the Classes unit providing sorted and unsorted string lists with name-value pair support, file I/O, and delimiter-separated text parsing capabilities.

Add.description: Adds a string to the list and returns its zero-based index.

Delete.description: Removes the string at the specified zero-based index from the list.

IndexOf.description: Returns the zero-based index of the first matching string, or -1 if not found.

Sort.description: Sorts the string list in ascending alphabetical order.

LoadFromFile.description: Loads the contents of a text file into the string list, one line per entry.

SaveToFile.description: Saves the contents of the string list to a text file, one line per entry.

Count.description: Returns the number of strings currently in the list.

Ord.description: Returns the ordinal value (integer position) of an ordinal-type expression, including characters, booleans, and enumeration values. For Char arguments, returns the ASCII code value.

Chr.description: Returns the character corresponding to the specified ASCII code value. The inverse of the Ord function, converting an integer byte value back to its character representation.

Inc.description: Increments an ordinal variable by 1 or by the optional amount n. More efficient than x := x + 1 as it generates optimized machine code for the increment operation.

Dec.description: Decrements an ordinal variable by 1 or by the optional amount n. More efficient than x := x - 1 as it generates optimized machine code for the decrement operation.

Number.description: Numeric data type for integer and floating-point values.

Object.description: Collection of key-value pairs (properties and methods).

null.description: Represents the intentional absence of any value.

undefined.description: Represents an uninitialized or missing value.

print.description: Output a value to the standard output stream.

length.description: Returns the number of elements in a collection or characters in a string.
